description: Letter from an unknown writer, probably in Alexandria, to Abū l-Khayr (?) b. Yūsuf, in Fustat, care of Abū l-Ḥasan al-Yahūdī al-Ṣā'igh in the market of the goldsmiths. The letter is in Judaeo-Arabic with the address in Arabic script. The writer is furious that the addressee traveled to Fustat, leaving behind debts in Alexandria and abandoning his wife and children, "may God not reward you," and repeatedly demands that he return immediately. The writer reports that the female slave left for al-Maḥalla 10 days ago, and a terrible rumor reached them that she is either critically ill (marīḍa ʿalā khuṭṭa) or dead. The addressee's wife is also "sick in her knee"; she had a fall, and now cannot sit up or stand up. The writer sends regards to many people including the addressee's sister and her children; Abū Naṣr and his son Salmān (?); and the old woman asks after her daughter. The addressee's other sister, Sitt Riyāḍ, his mother, and everyone in the house ask after the addressee's sister. ASE.
tags: slaves, illness: limb, illness: death, illness: bedbound, Arabic address, Illness, illness: marad, al-Mahalla, illness: slave, Illness: women's, Jariya, illness letter 969-1517, hostile letter, Illness: khutta
description_he: מכתב מכותב לא ידוע, כנראה באלכסנדריה, לאבו אל-ח'יר ב. יוסף בפוסטאט. ביהודית-ערבית. הכותב זועם שהנמען נסע לפוסטאט ועזב חובות באלכסנדריה ואת אשתו וילדיו. דורש שיחזור מיד
